dev.changes_summary: M1: Removed dead `sid = _get_session_id(data)` from `_evaluate_command`. M2: Added central fast-path `_check_git_allowlist` call in `_evaluate_command` conditioned on `not _looks_like_git_push(command)`; all 5 per-function calls in non-push evaluate-* functions retained as safety-net; no return added after _evaluate_push dispatch (OBJ-8 fix preserved). M3: Removed 6 secondary `check_and_consume_allowlist` calls (lines ~805, ~984, ~995, ~1007, ~1027, ~1110); replaced stale RELOCATED comment with accurate note; added documentation comment at global short-circuit explaining it is the sole PreToolUse allowlist match/approval call site. M4: Moved IS_SUBAGENT assignment to line 20 (after PYTHON_BIN resolution); removed inline parse from check_and_consume_allowlist; replaced _DO_IS_SUB inline parse in /do bypass block with IS_SUBAGENT reference; updated all stale comments.

tasks_completed.description: M1: Remove dead sid variable from _evaluate_command
tasks_completed.changes: Removed `sid = _get_session_id(data)` from _evaluate_command; the variable was assigned but never used in that function scope
tasks_completed.rationale: allow-1: dead assignment with no effect on correctness

tasks_completed.description: M2: Add central fast-path allowlist check in _evaluate_command dispatcher
tasks_completed.changes: Added `if not _looks_like_git_push(command) and _check_git_allowlist(command, data): return` as first check in _evaluate_command; added comment explaining push exemption; per-function calls retained; no return after push dispatch
tasks_completed.rationale: allow-2: structural enforcement at dispatcher level; per-function calls retained as safety-net for CLAUDE_PUSH_COMMAND_ACTIVE compound-command path (OBJ-8)

tasks_completed.description: M3: Remove 6 secondary check_and_consume_allowlist calls from pretool-bash-safety.sh
tasks_completed.changes: Removed secondary calls at ~lines 805 (systemctl), ~984 (git stash push/save), ~995 (bare git stash), ~1007 (git checkout wide-path), ~1027 (git restore --source wide-path), ~1110 (git revert); removed stale RELOCATED comment; added documentation comment at global short-circuit
tasks_completed.rationale: allow-3: global short-circuit at line 615 is unconditional and fires before all block rules; secondary calls had no live code path

tasks_completed.description: M4: Move IS_SUBAGENT to top of script, remove duplicate inline parses
tasks_completed.changes: Moved IS_SUBAGENT= to line 20 (after PYTHON_BIN resolution); removed 4-line inline parse block from check_and_consume_allowlist; replaced _DO_IS_SUB inline parse in /do bypass block with IS_SUBAGENT reference; updated stale comments at ~line 131 and ~line 1057
tasks_completed.rationale: allow-5: $INPUT is assigned once at line 6 and never reassigned; IS_SUBAGENT is valid for entire script lifetime; eliminates duplicate subprocess invocation per bypass check

git_rationale.root_cause_commit: N/A — not a regression; accumulated architectural debt
git_rationale.why_issue_occurred: Correct security additions made in isolation over multiple cycles without a centralised refactoring pass
git_rationale.how_fix_addresses_root: Centralized dispatcher fast-path (M2) provides structural enforcement; dead code removal (M1, M3) eliminates reader confusion; IS_SUBAGENT relocation (M4) eliminates duplicate subprocess invocation

dev.qa_notes: Key invariants to verify: (1) git push --force still blocked with /allow grant — _push_has_forbidden_ref_mutation at line 576 fires before _check_git_allowlist at line 583 in _evaluate_push; (2) CLAUDE_PUSH_COMMAND_ACTIVE compound push+commit scenario: central fast-path skipped (push detected), _evaluate_push allowed, _evaluate_commit's per-function call returns False, default-deny fires (AC8); (3) systemctl non-happy bypass still works via global short-circuit at line 618; (4) subagent firewall: IS_SUBAGENT at line 20 is read by check_and_consume_allowlist; subagents cannot consume grants.

self_verification.notes: Python AST parse: OK. Bash -n: OK. Grep confirms: (1) no sid= in _evaluate_command, (2) central fast-path with not _looks_like_git_push at line 661, (3) per-function calls at lines 455/480/602/615/648 retained, (4) _push_has_forbidden_ref_mutation at 576 before _check_git_allowlist at 583, (5) only one check_and_consume_allowlist invocation in bash (line 618), (6) IS_SUBAGENT= at line 20, (7) no remaining agent_id inline parse in bash except IS_SUBAGENT=

recommendations: Future: consolidate the 4 independent grant-read implementations (allow-6) — deferred to dedicated cycle per BA spec
